questions.question: What CSS property creates the admin sidebar layout?
questions.explanation: CSS Grid with grid-template-columns: 260px 1fr creates the sidebar+content layout.

questions.question: How do you make stat cards responsive without media queries?
questions.explanation: auto-fit with minmax creates responsive stat cards that wrap automatically.

questions.question: How do you create an active nav indicator in the sidebar?
questions.explanation: A colored left border on the active nav item creates the visual indicator.

questions.question: What is the purpose of sticky header in admin tables?
questions.explanation: Sticky table headers stay visible when scrolling through long data rows.

questions.question: How do you create CSS-only bar charts?
questions.explanation: CSS bar charts use divs with height representing the data value and background color.

questions.question: What do colored trend indicators communicate?
questions.explanation: Green typically indicates positive growth (up), red indicates decline (down).

questions.question: Which attribute indicates the current page in nav?
questions.explanation: aria-current='page' tells screen readers which nav item represents the current page.

questions.question: How do you handle mobile sidebar in admin dashboards?
questions.explanation: Mobile admin dashboards show sidebar as an overlay triggered by a hamburger menu.

questions.question: What font style is recommended for numeric data in dashboards?
questions.explanation: Monospace fonts align numbers properly, making columns of numbers easier to read and compare.

questions.question: How do you create zebra-striped table rows?
questions.explanation: The :nth-child pseudo-class alternates row background colors for readability.

questions.question: How do you create a data table with sortable column headers?
options: Styled headers with cursor: pointer and sort indicator icons
questions.explanation: Sortable columns use styled header elements with cursor pointer and visual sort direction indicators.

questions.question: What CSS approach creates pagination controls?
options: Flexbox row of page number buttons with highlighted active state
questions.explanation: A flexbox row of page number buttons with a distinct active page style is the standard pagination pattern.

questions.question: How do you create a status badge (e.g. Active, Pending, Completed)?
options: Span with color-coded background and border-radius
questions.explanation: Colored spans with border-radius create reusable status badges that communicate state at a glance.

questions.question: What CSS creates the card hover effect on dashboard widgets?
questions.explanation: An elevated box-shadow combined with a slight translateY lift on hover creates depth feedback.

questions.question: How do you create a search/filter bar for admin lists?
options: Flexbox row with input, select, and button
questions.explanation: A flexbox row combining text input, category select, and action button creates a functional filter bar.

questions.question: What is the best way to display user avatars in admin panels?
options: Circular images with object-fit: cover and fallback initials
questions.explanation: Circular images with object-fit: cover plus CSS-generated fallback initials ensure consistent avatar display.

questions.question: How do you create a notification dropdown in the header?
options: Absolute positioned dropdown triggered by click/toggle class
questions.explanation: An absolutely positioned dropdown panel toggled by a header icon button is the standard notification pattern.

questions.question: What CSS approach creates progress bars?
options: Outer div with background and inner div with width percentage and transition
questions.explanation: A container div with a colored inner div whose width is set as a percentage creates simple progress bars.

questions.question: How do you create quick action buttons in admin dashboards?
options: Icon buttons grouped in a flexbox row with tooltips
questions.explanation: Icon buttons with tooltips in a flexbox row create compact, recognizable quick action controls.

questions.question: How do you implement dark mode in an admin dashboard?
options: CSS custom properties toggled by a data-theme attribute on the root
questions.explanation: CSS custom properties with a toggled data-theme attribute provides the cleanest dark mode implementation.
